name: Houston Person
abstract: Houston Person (born November 10, 1934) is an American jazz tenor saxophonist and record producer. Although he has performed in the hard bop and swing genres, he is most experienced in and best known for his work in soul jazz.

interviews.title: Houston Person Part 1 interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 29, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]
Houston Person Part 2 interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 2, 1998 [interview 2 of 2]

concerts.label: Tribute to Oscar Peterson
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

works.t: It's Crazy But I'm in Love

about_quotes.speaker: Etta Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Houston got the group together... I listen to him. It hasn’t been bad so far.
about_quotes.doc_title: Etta Jones interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 2, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Junior Mance Part I
about_quotes.evidence: With Houston Person on saxophone and Alvin Queen on drums and Calvin Hill on bass. And we did it again with the horn.
about_quotes.doc_title: Junior Mance Part I interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, New York City, New York, July 27, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Big Nick Nicholas
about_quotes.evidence: His name was Person, he played some guitar, years before that, and I'd go by the pool hall and study

about_quotes.speaker: Urbie Green
about_quotes.evidence: I think you were talking to Houston Person earlier about if you just fall into it or what.
about_quotes.doc_title: Urbie Green interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 29, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Richard Wyands
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I can’t remember. Houston Person I think. [INTERVIEWEE] Is he on that one?
about_quotes.doc_title: Richard Wyands interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Tiny Grimes
about_quotes.evidence: How did you like recording Profound's Blue with Houston Person? It was all right.
